_fixture_notes: Zoning-only test fixture. Financial assumptions are intentionally minimal (industrial pre-development site, no rent roll, no NOI). Smoke tests use Belmont assumptions as financial substrate. Do not use for financial output testing.
deal_description: 42,420 SF warehouse on a 68,389 SF parcel in East Falls, Philadelphia, split between RSA-1 and RSA-5 residential zoning districts. Site burdened by two cell-tower encumbrances (American Tower easement; SBC Tower lease-to-easement). Opportunistic redevelopment thesis pursuing residential variance; CMA prepared three revised schemes (courtyard 95u, townhomes 88u, garden 84u).

zoning.zoning_district: Residential Single-Family Attached, District 5 (parcel is split-zoned RSA-1 / RSA-5; RSA-5 is the more permissive of the two — captured here as the controlling district pending a future schema field for split-zoning per Session 3 carry-forward)
zoning.source_notes: RSA-5 standards from Philadelphia Title 14; RSA-1 portion not separately captured in this fixture. The is_split_zoned / split_zoning_codes schema fields are deferred to a Session 1.8 micro-session per Session 3 carry-forward.

encumbrances.grantee: American Tower Asset Sub II, LLC
encumbrances.description: Recorded perpetual easement (post-conversion from a prior ground lease) burdening the parcel for a cell tower installation. Withdraws an exclusive area from buildable use and adds a companion access/utility easement running from Indian Queen Lane frontage to the tower pad.
encumbrances.notes: American Tower Easement and Assignment Agreement, Doc Id 53136944. Tower height and exclusive footprint must be reflected in any rebuild scenario.

encumbrances.description: Original cell tower ground lease with a conversion-to-easement provision; ROFR runs in favor of grantee. Companion encumbrance to the American Tower easement on a separate carrier installation.
encumbrances.notes: SBC Tower Holdings ground-lease document, Doc Id 52842941. Distinct from the American Tower easement above; both encumbrances must be addressed in scenario generation.
